column1: # Malayalam transliteration rules over SEDRA III vocalised ASCII tokens.
# VET AGAINST: Joju Jacob's East Syriac slides (Dukrana 2024). The deck's
# trilingual word tables define the real convention; every value below is a
# strawman for the machinery to chew on, nothing more.
#   @carrier  consonant contributes no letter; its vowel surfaces as an
#             independent vowel (അ ആ എ ഇ ഉ)
#   ^a ^o ^e ^i ^u   independent-vowel rows used after carriers / word-initial
# Known v0 artifacts, deliberate and flagged:
#  * Matres not contracted: ;eWuOE renders യെശുവ്, where the convention is
#    plainly different (cf. ഈശോ in Syro-Malabar usage). Vetting will fix.
#  * Spirantised (rukkakha) forms mostly collapse onto stops — Malayalam has
#    no ḡ/ḏ/ṯ distinction; rows flagged COLLISION need Joju's call.
#  * Emphatics ṭ/ṣ are drafted onto retroflex ട/ഷ — a grapheme convenience,
#    not a phonetic claim; needs the convention.
#  * Linea occultans not applied; silenced letters still print.
# TODO for the vetting session (user feedback, 2026-07-08 — Ameen finds
# the Malayalam output rough; concrete items for Joju's convention call):
#  * Word-final nasals/liquids: v0 prints ന്/ല്/ര് (base + virama); modern
#    Malayalam usage would write chillu ൻ/ൽ/ർ (e.g. ദെയ്ന് vs ദെയ്ൻ).
#    Decide chillu policy per letter and position.
#  * Mater contraction (the യെശുവ്/ഈശോ item above) is the single biggest
#    readability gap — long ū/ī spellings need the convention's word forms.
#  * Word-final ʿe/ʾalap after a vowel: does the convention drop, glide,
#    or carry them? v0 prints a bare വ്/യ് cluster that reads awkwardly.

column3: soft beth = v/w — COLLISION with waw (വ); needs Joju convention
soft gamal (ġ) — COLLISION with hard ഗ; Malayalam lacks the sound
soft dalath (ḏ) — COLLISION with hard ദ
waw; mater not contracted in v0
COLLISION with semkath (സ); z has no separate Malayalam letter
heth (ḥ) — COLLISION with he (ഹ); needs Joju convention
teth (ṭ) drafted onto retroflex ട — needs convention
yudh; mater not contracted in v0
soft kaph (ḵ) drafted onto ഖ — COLLISION with qaph draft; needs convention
ayin drafted as carrier (no Malayalam equivalent) — needs convention
sadhe (ṣ) drafted onto retroflex ഷ — needs convention
qaph — cf. ഖുർആൻ for Arabic qāf; still needs convention
soft taw (ṯ) — COLLISION with hard ത; alternative ഥ; needs Joju convention
pthaha = inherent vowel; empty output is correct
rbasa; zlama split not recoverable from SEDRA's 'e'
